one_line: Imperial Chola king of Thanjavur, patron of the Rajarajesvara (Brihadisvara) temple and conqueror of land and sea.

appearance.age_look: Late 30s to early 40s, tall and broad-shouldered with a powerful, disciplined build.
appearance.face: Oval face with strong jawline, medium-brown South Indian complexion, straight nose, sharp arched brows, small tilak of white ash with a central red dot on the forehead, default expression calm and commanding with a faint confident half-smile.
appearance.hair: Black hair grown long and oiled, pulled back into a high, thick topknot at the back crown of the head; sides neatly smoothed; wears a simple gold band circlet resting above the forehead and a narrow strip of white cloth tied around the base of the topknot.
appearance.costume: Upper body mostly bare but for layered gold necklaces and a fitted, short-sleeved, deep-maroon silk angavastra draped across the left shoulder and under the right arm; lower body wrapped in a finely pleated silk veshti/dhoti in muted cream with a broad maroon border and thin gold stripe, tied in a tight knot at the front with pleats falling cleanly between the legs; he wears a plain but solid gold armlet on each upper arm, multiple slim gold bangles on the right wrist, a heavier bangle on the left, simple round gold ear ornaments with a small pendant drop, and an ornate yet compact waist belt (oddiyanam) of gold with subtle temple motifs holding the veshti; feet in plain brown leather sandals; no cape or exaggerated armor, but a short, dark-red cloth girdle over the waist belt hinting at military readiness.
appearance.signature: A long, slightly curved steel sword with a plain broad blade, a dark wooden hilt with simple gold ferrule and pommel, worn in an unadorned brown leather scabbard hanging from a functional belt at his left hip, always held or worn with the hilt angled forward for quick draw.

ref_desc: Rajaraja Chola I is a tall, muscular, medium-brown man with an oval face, arched brows, ash-and-red tilak, long black hair in a high topknot with a gold circlet and white tie, wearing a deep-maroon angavastra, cream veshti with maroon-gold border, simple gold ornaments, brown sandals, and carrying a plain curved steel sword with wooden hilt in a brown leather scabbard, with maroon, cream, gold, dark brown and black as dominant colours.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Rajaraja Chola I: a tall, broad-shouldered South Indian king in his late 30s–40s with an oval face, strong jaw, medium-brown complexion, straight nose, sharp arched brows, and a calm, commanding half-smile; he has a small ash-and-red tilak on his forehead and long black hair oiled and pulled into a high, thick topknot at the back crown, the sides smoothed, with a simple gold band circlet above the forehead and a narrow white cloth strip tied around the base of the topknot. His upper body is mostly bare except for layered gold necklaces and a fitted, short-sleeved deep-maroon silk angavastra draped over the left shoulder and under the right arm; his lower body is wrapped in a finely pleated muted-cream silk veshti/dhoti with a broad deep-maroon border and thin gold stripe, tied in a tight front knot with clean central pleats. Show plain but solid gold armlets on both upper arms, slim gold bangles on the right wrist, a heavier bangle on the left, simple round gold earrings with small pendant drops, and an ornate yet compact gold waist belt (oddiyanam) with subtle temple motifs securing the veshti, plus a short dark-red cloth girdle over the belt. Put plain brown leather sandals on his feet. At his left hip, show a long, slightly curved steel sword with a plain broad blade and dark wooden hilt with a simple gold ferrule and pommel, resting in an unadorned brown leather scabbard with the hilt angled forward; use deep maroon, muted cream, gold, dark brown and black as the dominant colours, and present him front, side and back in consistent costume, hair, face and weapon details.
